title: Tiered Public Pricing with Showcase Anchoring (primitive)

statusHistory.reason: src/components/Pricing.tsx renders 4 tiers with concrete prices. Tier 0 card links to /tier-0 self-service redirect. Tier 2 visually anchored as the demo's tier. Showcase wall doubles as tier reference (filterable by tier chip).

problem: "Cheap" without numbers is rhetoric — every visitor self-imagines a different price. The tiered-public-pricing primitive shows actual prices per tier (Tier 0 self-service / Tier 1 ~$300 / Tier 2 ~$1500 / Tier 3 ~$3000+) with each tier linked to actual past showcase examples that demonstrate what that tier looks like in practice. The showcase wall doubles as the living tier reference. Anchors expectations in 5 seconds. Counter to most consulting where pricing is hidden behind "let's have a conversation." The whole pitch depends on cheapness being real, not negotiated.
analysis: Per-tier public pricing display where each tier shows a concrete price + a 2-line scope definition + a link to actual past showcases that exemplify that tier in practice. Tier 0 = self-service redirect (free). Tier 1 = ~$300, smallest paid projects. Tier 2 = ~$1500, primary tier (Sunday Night Dread sits here). Tier 3 = ~$3000+, larger one-off engagements. Once shipped, every showcase is automatically tier-tagged so the wall doubles as the tier reference. Anchors visitor expectations in 5 seconds. Counter to standard consulting where pricing is hidden behind a 'let's have a conversation' tactic.

decisions.rationale: Insight #24 — without numbers, every visitor self-imagines a different price. The whole 'cheap' positioning depends on cheapness being visible, not negotiated.
decisions.recommendation: Concrete dollar amounts per tier. No 'starting at' or 'depending on scope' fudge. If a project does not fit a tier cleanly, custom-quote it post-triage but anchor the conversation to the public tiers.

decisions.decision: Each tier links to actual showcase examples that demonstrate that tier
decisions.rationale: Insight #38 — abstract tier descriptions ('Starter / Pro / Premium') do not anchor expectations the way 'here are 4 actual Tier 1 projects we shipped' does.
decisions.recommendation: Per-tier links to filtered showcase wall. Day 1, only the Demo Session is tagged Tier 2; over time, the wall populates each tier's bucket. After 6 months each tier should have at least 1 example.

decisions.decision: Tier 0 is a self-service redirect, not a paid product
decisions.rationale: Insight #27 — graceful off-ramp for budget-mismatched clients. Free templates and similar-showcase links for problems too small to charge for.
decisions.recommendation: Tier 0 is implemented in feat-self-service-redirect, not here. The pricing page links to it as 'too small? start here.'

successCriteria:
- Pricing page shows concrete dollar amounts per tier
- Each tier links to filtered showcase examples of that tier (where shipped projects exist)
- Showcase wall items are auto-tagged with their tier on publish
- Visitor can calibrate expectations in <5 seconds without scrolling past the pricing block
- No 'contact us for pricing' anywhere in the funnel
- Reusable as a pattern for any pricing-transparent service business

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Initial fill from brainstorming — primitive captures the public pricing + showcase-as-tier-reference principles (Insights #24, #38).

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Instantiated as src/components/Pricing.tsx. Bilingual copy in src/i18n.ts under FR.pricing / EN.pricing. Tier 2 visually anchored (border-color: var(--accent)) because Sunday Night Dread sits at Tier 2 — visitors see the demo's tier first.

implementation: Renders 4 tiers (Tier 0 self-service / Tier 1 ≈$300 / Tier 2 ≈$1500 / Tier 3 ≈$3000+) as cards in a CSS grid. Each card shows tier name, price, scope (1-2 lines), and an 'after' line describing post-ship support. Tier 2 has the 'tier--anchor' modifier class which adds an accent border — Sunday Night Dread is the Tier 2 example, so visitors anchor expectations on the demo's tier. Copy lives in src/i18n.ts (FR.pricing.tiers + EN.pricing.tiers). The showcase wall doubles as the living tier reference (Insight #38) — clicking a showcase reveals the full engagement at that tier. No inline tier→showcase filter yet (the wall has tier filter chips that visitors can use).
userGuide: Edit prices/scopes: src/i18n.ts under FR.pricing.tiers (and the EN mirror). Each tier is { name, price, scope, after, anchor? }. Setting anchor:true highlights the tier visually. The showcase wall at /#showcases doubles as proof — visitors can filter the wall by tier (chips) to see actual past projects of each price point.
knownLimitations: Pricing copy lives in i18n.ts, not in this primitive's data — moving it here would let multiple sites reuse the pricing component without touching i18n
No automatic per-tier showcase filter link from the pricing block (visitors have to scroll to the wall)
Tier 0 is described but not yet linked to feat-self-service-redirect (which doesn't exist as a built page yet)
